subtitle: Access case #69001638 overdue; Friday meeting prep critical
context: James Salas Guillen is absent on annual leave and will return Thursday 18 June, blocking progress on the DSE system data upload to Cardinus that went live 3 June and the automated PeopleXD interface monitoring he manages. Athena Artuso is absent all day Friday 12 June. Sophie Levy from Cority has been waiting 72 hours (since 8 June) without response regarding the scoping session she mentioned in April. Ann-Marie Topliss at The Access Group created FlexPoints case #69001638 for the PeopleXD Insight Reporting quote request, with your FlexPoints expiring by 29 June 2026 - this needs immediate attention before the deadline. The critical deadline this week is your Friday 12 June 10:00-12:00 meeting with Conor O'Brien on Organisational Hierarchy & Payroll Company Set-Up via Microsoft Teams, where you'll need to present the draft REF2029 load file you prepared. Open first: Ann-Marie Topliss's email about Access Support case #69001638 regarding the Holiday Records Reports quote, as Mike West is out of office and you're now the primary contact for this deliverable.

prioritiesToday.text: Web pages and URL linking — job posting updates
prioritiesToday.description: Internal Mobility Principles page moved to a new OxIntranet URL. Three pages still link to the old location: main ops intranet page, professional services search page, and individual vacancy application pages. New URL confirmed live on 3 June. Old page expected to be retired 23/24 June. Agreed approach: update all links to the new URL, use hyperlinked text (“IM Principles” / “internal mobility principles”) rather than displaying the full long URL, test before making any live changes, and check whether links can open in a new tab. Updates planned for 22 June ahead of old page retirement. Follow-up with Laura scheduled 15–16 June.
actions: [03 Jun 2026] Meeting with Laura Porter — reviewed three pages needing link updates.
[03 Jun 2026] New URL confirmed live later that day: https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/internal-mobility-principles.aspx
[TODO — 15/16 Jun] Reconnect with Laura Porter as planned.
[TODO — 22 Jun] Test new URL, update links on all three identified pages, replace visible long URLs with hyperlinked text, check whether links open in a new tab.
[TODO] Email Laura with updates if changes are made before they next meet.
[MONITOR] Old page retirement — expected 23/24 June.

prioritiesToday.text: College Data in PXD Project — set up REF2029 pay admin codes in UOXU
prioritiesToday.description: Pay admin buy codes need to be set up in PeopleXD for DTP1092. Blocked pending Simon's assessment of what already exists at level 6 in the system. Simon will create missing reference data (Division 901, subdivision, levels 4 and 5) before Kevin proceeds. Context: during Kevin's leave, two org structure changes were incomplete — full department structures had not been created down to pay admin level. Michael had to create missing codes ad hoc when staff requests couldn't be processed. Root cause is linked to the documentation/process gap (see t006). No action from Kevin until Simon gives the green light.
actions: [08 Jun] Confirmed blocked — waiting for Simon's level 6 assessment.
[09 Jun 2026] Context confirmed at FA Catch-up: ad hoc codes created by Michael during Kevin's leave as workaround. Last two structure changes were incomplete at pay admin level.
[AWAITING] Simon to complete assessment and create missing reference data (Division 901, subdivision, levels 4 and 5).
[TODO — after Simon] Set up pay admin buy codes once Simon confirms ready.

[10 Jun 2026] Simon (Teams, after 1-1): department codes for all colleges and halls already exist in reference data - no department code work needed. Asked Kevin to set up all 40 REF2029 Pay Administered By codes in UOXU. Full code list: docs/REF2029_pay_admin_codes.md.
[10 Jun 2026] Simon created in UOXU: Division Code 901 (Colleges and Halls), USER_CODE1 ZSD901, USER_CODE2 Z90101, MANAGEMENT_UNITs Z901 (Colleges) and Z902 (Permanent Private Halls).
[TODO] Set up all 40 Pay Administered By codes in UOXU (list in docs/REF2029_pay_admin_codes.md).
[TODO] Raise change request - include Simon’s created items (Division 901, ZSD901, Z90101, Z901, Z902) and note the Division Code blocker below.
[AWAITING] Division Code cannot be added until linked to a Company Code - blocked until Conor walks through the config on Fri 12 Jun.

[10 Jun 2026] Simon has found that some reference data codes were too long and adjusted them; Kevin needs to review the changes to confirm they are acceptable before proceeding with the setup. (email: Simon Burford - RE: College staff in PeopleXD proposal)

[10 Jun 2026] Simon identified that proposed org structure codes for the multi-company setup were too long and has made adjustments in the test environment; Kevin should review these changes. (email: Simon Burford - RE: #External# Re: University of Oxford: FP 68261303 - Multi Company Setup)

[11 Jun 2026] Kevin has located Pay Administered By values in UOXU and can see what exists at level 6; ready to progress once Simon confirms missing reference data creation. (email: Kevin (sent to: Conor O'Brien) - Re: Organisational Hierarchy & Payroll Company Set-Up)

prioritiesToday.text: Review Holiday Records Duty reports from Athena
prioritiesToday.description: Athena Artuso has sent a full review of Holiday Records Duty requirements with an introductory section covering what is actually required. Kevin has asked her to send this through and needs to review it to understand the scope and requirements for this duty.
actions: [10 Jun 2026] Created from inbox suggestion (email from Athena Artuso)
[10 Jun 2026] Kevin asked Athena to send through the requirements review document to examine scope and requirements (sent: Re: Holiday Records Duty - proposed reports).
[AWAITING] Athena to send the Holiday Records Duty requirements review document.

[10 Jun 2026] Athena has sent the full Holiday Records Duty review with introductory section following management team meeting discussion; Kevin needs to review this document. (email: Athena Artuso - RE: Holiday Records Duty - proposed reports)

[11 Jun 2026] Athena has sent the full Holiday Records Duty review with scope and requirements as requested; Kevin can now proceed to review. (email: Athena Artuso - RE: Holiday Records Duty - proposed reports)

[11 Jun 2026] Kevin confirmed willingness to review Athena's Holiday Records Duty proposed reports and asked Athena to send through the document. (email: Kevin (sent to: Simon Burford; Athena Artuso) - Re: Holiday Records Duty - proposed reports)

[11 Jun 2026] Kevin has reviewed the revised three-report specification and confirmed it aligns with requirements; now moving to quote stage with Access Group. (email: Kevin (sent to: Athena Artuso; Simon Burford) - Re: Holiday Records Duty - proposed reports)

prioritiesWeek.description: Smart notes in Cority CoreEHS+ (GX2) do not allow edits after save and close. Raised November 2025, dev ref CCC-19664. Cority's official position (confirmed 08 Jun 2026 by Mehran Riaz, support leadership): this is an INTENDED feature — not a bug or missing feature. Cority classify the request to allow post-save edits as an enhancement, and it is NOT on their current roadmap. No timeline will be provided. Beta/early access ('enhanced access support') requires Premium Support, which Oxford does not have. Oxford has no named account manager — we are in a pool. Escalation route: support case owner → support leadership (done). If account/contract items needed, Cority sales team can be asked to reach out. Quality PUG on Tue 16 Jun at 2pm remains the formal pressure point.
actions: [Nov 2025] Ticket 00476764 raised by James — smart notes cannot be edited after save/close.
[Nov–Jun] Multiple 'still working on it' updates from Freeda Franks (support analyst). Dev ref CCC-19664 assigned.
[08 Jun 2026] Kevin escalated formally — requested timeline, clarification on cost comment, named account manager.

[08 Jun 2026] Mehran Riaz (support leadership) responded: feature is INTENDED behaviour, classified as enhancement, NOT on roadmap. No timeline. Premium Support required for beta access (Oxford does not have it). No named account manager — pool model. Sales team available if needed.
[MONITOR] Quality PUG — Tue 16 Jun, 2pm. Raise CCC-19664 formally. Use Mehran's response as evidence that standard escalation is exhausted.
[TODO — before PUG] Prepare talking points: 6+ months open, intentional feature reclassification, no roadmap commitment, no account manager, Premium Support paywall.
[TODO — after PUG] Decide whether to pursue via sales/account route or accept as a known limitation and communicate to stakeholders.

prioritiesWeek.text: Chemistry bulk delete — get details from Julie, pass to Reenu
prioritiesWeek.description: Bulk deletion of appraisal cycle assignments in PeopleXD for Chemistry department. Appraisal cycle name: '(DO NOT USE) CDR2025Chemistry'. 5,568 staff incorrectly assigned and need to be removed. AG contact: Reenu Delaney (cases 68974493 / 67938206). Payment via flex points (£525, approved by Marie). Julie Hickman has run the affected employee report and will share the export via OneDrive.
actions: [Oct 2025] Case 67938206 raised — Chemistry bulk delete of appraisal assignments.
[Apr 2026] Flex points agreed as payment route — case 68974493 raised.
[02 Jun 2026] Marie handed case to Kevin as lead.
[03 Jun 2026] Reenu Delaney requested appraisal cycle name and affected employee list.
[09 Jun 2026] Email sent to Julie Hickman requesting details.
[09 Jun 2026] Julie confirmed: cycle name '(DO NOT USE) CDR2025Chemistry', 5,568 staff to be removed. Report run by Julie — OneDrive export incoming.
[AWAITING] Julie to share OneDrive export of affected employee list.
[TODO — on receipt] Forward cycle name + employee list export to Reenu Delaney at AG to action the deletion.
[TODO] Reply to Julie confirming details received and next steps.

[10 Jun 2026] Julie Hickman is on annual leave 10 June; queries should be redirected per her auto-reply instructions. (email: Julie Hickman - Automatic reply: Access Support Team | 68974493 - New Case)
[10 Jun 2026] Access Group case 68978576 has been updated; monitor for resolution progress. (email: Access Support - Access Support Team | 68978576 - Your Case Has Been Updated)

[10 Jun 2026] Kevin replied to Julie Hickman confirming AG are ready to proceed on Chemistry bulk delete; two items needed from Kevin's team before they can progress. (email: Kevin (sent to: Julie Hickman) - Re: Access Support Team | 68974493 - New Case)

[10 Jun 2026] Kevin confirmed receipt of export from Julie Hickman for Chemistry bulk delete and is passing details across to Reenu Delaney to progress the AG case. (email: Kevin (sent to: Julie Hickman) - Re: Access Support Team | 68974493 - New Case)

prioritiesWeek.text: Flex points plan before Marie returns
prioritiesWeek.description: 2,935 flex points remaining with expiry approximately early July 2026. Simon and Marie have already agreed a plan will be ready when Marie returns from leave. Chemistry bulk deletion (£525, AG, approved by Marie) is already committed. Remaining points need to be allocated before expiry — options discussed include payroll training and manager holiday compliance reports. Athena has sent an email with holiday compliance requirements that needs to be passed to Michael to assess against current system capability.
actions: [08 Jun] Simon to send Athena's holiday compliance requirements email — awaiting.
[09 Jun 2026] Team asked for flex point usage ideas by end of week at FA Team Catch-up.
[TODO] Once Athena's email received from Simon, pass to Michael — ask him to pull screenshots confirming system meets holiday provision requirements (holiday calculator evidence included).
[TODO] Talk to Michael about additional flex point ideas — get as many quotes to AG as possible.

prioritiesWeek.description: The shared drive was moved to SharePoint but guidance documentation was not updated to reflect the new location. Only Kevin and Lee were managing the migration, so other team members did not know where to find documents. The documents exist and are in the right place — they are just not signposted correctly. Simon has raised this and Kevin needs to respond in writing before recommending an alignment meeting to bring the whole team up to speed. Michael also needs to be looped in.

Created a single point of failure between Kevin and Lee — others didn't know where to look. Documents exist and are correctly placed; signposting is the issue.

prioritiesWeek.text: DSE data feed issues — post go-live
prioritiesWeek.description: DSE data feed went live on Wednesday 3 June 2026. Three issues emerged immediately after go-live that require resolution. 1. SBS opted out of the main DSE system (they have their own) but were included in the initial feed — Azure integrations team is handling their exclusion. 2. Contractors are missing from PeopleXD and therefore absent from the feed despite having DSE requirements — a manual addition process is needed. 3. A small number of users do not have university email addresses, preventing standard access — James is investigating with the supplier. James is on leave until 18 June. Kevin to monitor and flag any escalations. It was noted as disappointing that the SBS exclusion was a known requirement but was not built into the original feed.
actions: [03 Jun 2026] DSE data feed went live — three issues identified post go-live.

[08 Jun 2026] Issues reviewed in H&S Roadmap meeting: SBS exclusion (Azure team handling), contractors missing from PXD feed (manual process needed), users without university emails (James investigating with supplier).
